refnamediv.refpurpose: Set a callback function to run on server failure

refentry.refsect1:
&reftitle.description;
boolMemcache::setFailureCallback callablefailure_callback
boolmemcache_set_failure_callback Memcachememcache callablefailure_callback
Memcache::setFailureCallback registers a callback function which is invoked whenever a server from the connection pool fails. This provides a single place to react to a failing server, for instance to log the error or to remove the server from the pool, and it applies to every server of the pool. The callback replaces any callback previously registered on the same connection.

&reftitle.parameters;
failure_callback
The callback to run when a server of the pool fails. It is called with five arguments: the host name of the failed server (string), its TCP port (int), its UDP port (int), the error message (string or &null; when none is available), and the error number (int).
Passing &null; removes the callback currently registered on the connection.
